title: N-Queens
prompt:
The N-Queens puzzle is the problem of placing `n` queens on an `n x n` chessboard such that no two queens attack each other.

Given an integer `n`, return the number of distinct solutions to the N-Queens puzzle.

A queen can attack horizontally, vertically, and diagonally.

examples.output:
2
Explanation: There are two distinct solutions to the 4-queens puzzle.

1
Explanation: A single queen on a 1x1 board is the only solution.

notesForAI.coreIdea: Backtracking row by row. For each row, try placing a queen in each column. Track occupied columns, main diagonals (row - col), and anti-diagonals (row + col) using sets. If a position is safe, place the queen and recurse to the next row. When all rows are filled, increment the solution count.
commonBugs: Incorrect diagonal identification: main diagonal is row-col, anti-diagonal is row+col
Forgetting to remove the queen from tracking sets during backtracking
Only checking column conflicts but missing diagonal conflicts
notesForAI.expectedComplexity: O(n!) time with pruning, O(n) space for tracking sets
